mission.title: harbor: Cannot change tag retention schedule - v1.10.1
mission.description: Cannot change tag retention schedule - v1.10.1. This issue affects 12+ users.

steps.title: Identify harbor troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `500 {"code":10006,"message":"stop job failed with error","details":"mismatch job status for stopping job: 680d1e1161c9bc`

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
Whenever I try to update the schedule for tag retention rules, I get an internal server error.

steps.title: Upgrade Harbor to fix tag retention scheduling
steps.description:
Upgrade Harbor to a version that includes the tag retention schedule fix:
```bash
# Update the Harbor Helm repo
helm repo update harbor

# Upgrade Harbor
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml

# Verify pods restart successfully
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-jobservice -n harbor
```
After upgrading, you should be able to modify tag retention schedules without errors.

steps.title: Confirm Cannot change tag retention schedule - v1.10.1 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `500 {"code":10006,"message":"stop job failed with error","details":"mismatch job status for stopping job: 680d1e1161c9bc` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary: Tag retention schedule changes fail in Harbor v1.10.1 because the system attempts to stop a running job that has already completed (in Success, Error, or Stopped state), which returns an error. The fix returns nil instead of an error when trying to stop a job that is already in a final state, and improves the periodic job unschedule function to handle this case gracefully.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
